description.current: Capture immutable 'before' evidence before code changes. Proves changes improved targets without regressing existing functionality. Use for refactor, migration, dependency update, bug fix.
issues: Description is outcome-focused ('Proves changes...') but doesn't explain WHAT the skill does: 'Capture baseline evidence before code changes'
Missing key constraint: baseline must be captured BEFORE editing starts — this is a gate/blocker, not just best practice

issues.description: Extreme underweight: 94 lines total, only 4 steps, mostly references. Skill leaves all detailed capture commands to bundled references (baseline-capture-commands.md, regression-comparison-template.md) but doesn't include platform-specific examples IN the main skill for users who don't want to switch to references
Step 2 (Identify journeys) defers to 'create-validation-plan' skill without any bootstrap guidance — what if user doesn't have a plan yet? No inline checklist to get started

Rules section (lines 70-77) uses MUST/MUST NOT in all caps 5x (lines 71, 72, 73, 74, 75) without explaining WHY each rule matters. 'BASELINE MUST be captured before any code changes' lacks context: why is this a blocker?
Regression detection table (lines 57-62) is helpful but lacks examples: what does 'broken in baseline too' look like in practice? How do you distinguish pre-existing from introduced?
Post-Change Comparison section (lines 64-68) is 5 lines and defers everything to references/regression-comparison-template.md — user must leave the skill to understand what to do

strengths: Crystal clear purpose (lines 15-20): explains why baseline matters ('without a baseline, every claim of no regressions is unverifiable')
When to Use table (lines 26-34) is excellent — differentiates 'Baseline Required' from 'Optional', practical scenarios
Immutability rule (line 72) is well-intentioned — prevents accidental overwrites
Security Policy section (lines 79-81) is mature — warns about PII in evidence
Bundled references are well-named: regression-comparison-template.md, baseline-capture-commands.md

bundled_resources.missing_scripts_opportunity: Step 3 (capture evidence) delegates to references/baseline-capture-commands.md but could include platform-detection logic: 'Detect platform type (Web/API/iOS/CLI) from signals (package.json, .xcodeproj, Cargo.toml). For each platform, see references/baseline-capture-commands.md and run capture commands.' — would be a bundled script or logic diagram

top_fixes: Expand Scope section to include a simple platform detection checklist: 'Is this a Web app (React/Vue/Next)? API server (Node/Python/Go)? iOS app? CLI tool? See references/baseline-capture-commands.md for your platform's capture commands.'

Add inline example after Step 2: 'Example journeys for a React app: Homepage load, Login flow, Dashboard data fetch, Settings save. For an API: GET /health, POST /users (create), GET /users/:id (read), PUT /users/:id (update), DELETE /users/:id (delete).'

Explain WHY each rule (lines 70-77): 'BASELINE MUST be captured before any code changes — once you start editing, the before state is permanently lost.' 'Baseline evidence is immutable — if you need to update baseline after changes, save to baseline-v2/ to preserve the original.'

Move regression detection logic to a subsection with examples: 'CRITICAL: Feature worked in baseline (evidence: page loaded, API returned 200), now fails (evidence: 500 error). Must fix. MEDIUM: Lighthouse score 85 → 78 (performance regression but not broken). HIGH: Worked before, broken now = must fix.'

Expand Post-Change Comparison (lines 64-68) with mini-example: 'After your changes, re-run the same capture commands. Save to e2e-evidence/ (NOT baseline/). Open regression-comparison-template.md to structure your findings. Each journey gets a verdict: NO REGRESSION, IMPROVED, or REGRESSION.'

Rewrite description to lead with action: 'Before you change code, capture immutable evidence of existing behavior. After changes, re-capture and compare to prove you improved targets without breaking anything else.'
Create a mini-bootstrap guide: if user has no validation plan yet, provide a 5-minute checklist (e.g., 'List every route/endpoint/screen. Pick top 3. Capture screenshots/API responses. Done.').

test_prompts.prompt: I'm about to refactor our login form. It currently works fine. How do I capture baseline evidence before I start editing so I can prove the refactor didn't break anything?
test_prompts.expected_output: Before editing: Take a screenshot of the login form (e2e-evidence/baseline/login-form.png). Try a valid login and capture the response JSON (e2e-evidence/baseline/login-success.json). Try invalid credentials and capture the error (e2e-evidence/baseline/login-error.json). Save a summary to e2e-evidence/baseline/assessment.md. Now you can refactor. After refactor: re-run the same 3 captures and compare — if the form still works and API responses are identical, NO REGRESSION.

test_prompts.prompt: I want to update our Express API from v4 to v5. What baseline evidence do I capture before the upgrade, and how do I prove the upgrade didn't break anything?
test_prompts.expected_output: Baseline before upgrade: Run all 6 API validation steps (health check, CRUD cycle, auth, error responses, pagination, rate limiting if applicable). Save every curl response to e2e-evidence/baseline/api-*. After upgrade: re-run the same 6 steps, save to e2e-evidence/api-after-upgrade/*. Compare: status codes, response structure, auth behavior. If all match, no regression. If any differ, investigate.

test_prompts.prompt: I found a bug in the search results page — it's returning duplicates. This is a pre-existing bug (it was broken before my changes). Should I capture this in the baseline?
test_prompts.expected_output: Yes. Capture the current broken state in baseline/ (e.g., 'search-duplicates.json shows results array has duplicates'). Document it in baseline/assessment.md as 'Known Issues: search returns duplicates (pre-existing, not a regression)'. After you fix the bug, capture the fixed state in e2e-evidence/, compare, and show IMPROVED (was broken, now fixed). The baseline proves it existed before your work.

test_prompts.prompt: I realized I captured baseline evidence AFTER I'd already made some code changes. Is my baseline still valid?
test_prompts.expected_output: No. Baseline must be captured BEFORE any editing starts. Your baseline now reflects mixed states (some old code, some new). Delete it and re-capture by reverting to the original code, capturing fresh, then reapplying your changes. Baseline is only useful if it represents the true 'before' state.
